title: Klasea tanaitica
intro: Klasea tanaitica, also known as Tanaitian Klasea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Klasea tanaitica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and respiratory tract infections.
taxonomy: Klasea tanaitica is classified within the family Boraginaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-not. This family consists of around 100 genera and over 2,000 species. Klasea tanaitica is further classified within the genus Klasea, which is a genus of flowering plants native to the Middle East and Asia.
